Title: First-time access flow
Preconditions: User has never accessed the app before.
Test Steps:
1. User accesses the app.
2. System checks if it's the first access.
3. System starts the application.
4. System checks if the user is already registered.
5. Since it's the first access, the user is not registered.
6. System directs the user to the registration process.
7. User completes registration.
8. System redirects the user to the home screen.
Expected Result: User successfully registers and lands on the home screen.

Title: Returning user login flow
Preconditions: User has previously accessed the app and is registered.
Test Steps:
1. User accesses the app.
2. System checks if it's the first access.
3. System starts the application.
4. System checks if the user is already registered.
5. Since the user is registered, the system prompts for login.
6. User enters valid credentials.
7. System verifies credentials with the server.
8. Server confirms credentials with the database.
9. Server sends confirmation back to the application.
10. Application informs the user that login is successful.
11. System redirects the user to the home screen.
Expected Result: User logs in successfully and lands on the home screen.

Title: Invalid login attempt
Preconditions: User has previously accessed the app but entered incorrect credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User accesses the app.
2. System checks if it's the first access.
3. System starts the application.
4. System checks if the user is already registered.
5. Since the user is registered, the system prompts for login.
6. User enters invalid credentials.
7. System verifies credentials with the server.
8. Server fails to confirm credentials with the database.
9. Server sends failure message back to the application.
10. Application informs the user that login failed.
Expected Result: User receives a login failure message and remains on the login screen.

Title: Route in progress flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and has started a route.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the home screen.
2. System checks if a route is in progress.
3. Since a route is in progress, the system prompts to start the route.
4. User confirms to start the route.
5. System checks if the route is finished.
6. Since the route is not finished, the system returns to the home screen.
Expected Result: User stays on the home screen until the route is completed.

Title: Route completion flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and has completed a route.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the home screen.
2. System checks if a route is in progress.
3. Since a route is in progress, the system prompts to start the route.
4. User confirms to start the route.
5. System checks if the route is finished.
6. Since the route is finished, the system calculates emissions.
7. System returns to the home screen.
Expected Result: User sees emissions calculated and lands on the home screen.

Title: Change section flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to change sections.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the home screen.
2. System checks if a route is in progress.
3. Since no route is in progress, the system asks if the user wants to change sections.
4. User confirms to change sections.
5. System displays the horizontal menu.
6. User selects a section.
7. System returns to the home screen.
Expected Result: User sees the horizontal menu and lands back on the home screen after selecting a section.

Title: Weekly report visualization flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to view statistics.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the statistics page.
2. System prompts to visualize a weekly report.
3. User confirms to visualize the report.
4. System displays the report.
5. System asks if the user wants to change sections.
6. User confirms to change sections.
7. System displays the horizontal menu.
8. User selects a section.
9. System returns to the statistics page.
Expected Result: User views the weekly report and lands back on the statistics page after selecting a section.

Title: No weekly report visualization flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and does not want to view a weekly report.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the statistics page.
2. System prompts to visualize a weekly report.
3. User declines to visualize the report.
4. System displays the horizontal menu.
5. System asks if the user wants to change sections.
6. User confirms to change sections.
7. System displays the horizontal menu.
8. User selects a section.
9. System returns to the statistics page.
Expected Result: User sees the horizontal menu and lands back on the statistics page after selecting a section.

Title: Profile view and data update flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to update their profile.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile view.
2. System displays personal data.
3. System asks if the user wants to update their account.
4. User confirms to update the account.
5. System prompts to enter new data.
6. User enters new data.
7. System updates the data.
8. System returns to the profile view.
Expected Result: User sees updated personal data on the profile view.

Title: Logout flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to log out.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile view.
2. System asks if the user wants to log out.
3. User confirms to log out.
4. System performs logout.
5. System returns to the profile view.
Expected Result: User is logged out and sees the profile view.

Title: Change section flow from profile view
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to change sections from the profile view.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile view.
2. System asks if the user wants to change sections.
3. User confirms to change sections.
4. System displays the horizontal menu.
5. User selects a section.
6. System returns to the profile view.
Expected Result: User sees the horizontal menu and lands back on the profile view after selecting a section.

Title: Successful login sequence
Preconditions: User has valid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates login process.
2. Application requests credentials.
3. Application sends credentials to the server.
4. Server requests data verification from the database.
5. Database returns data found.
6. Server compares data with the credentials.
7. Server confirms successful login to the application.
8. Application informs the user that login is successful.
Expected Result: User logs in successfully and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Failed login sequence
Preconditions: User has invalid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates login process.
2. Application requests credentials.
3. Application sends credentials to the server.
4. Server requests data verification from the database.
5. Database returns data not found.
6. Server confirms failed login to the application.
7. Application informs the user that login failed.
Expected Result: User receives a login failure message.

Title: Route calculation and emissions display sequence
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to start a route.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a route.
2. Application sends data for calculation to the server.
3. Server adds the route to the database.
4. Database returns the new route.
5. Server calculates emissions and returns data to the application.
6. Application displays CO2 emissions to the user.
Expected Result: User sees the calculated CO2 emissions for the route.

Title: Route calculation failure sequence
Preconditions: User is logged in but the route cannot be processed.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a route.
2. Application sends data for calculation to the server.
3. Server attempts to add the route to the database.
4. Database fails to process the route.
5. Server returns an error message to the application.
6. Application informs the user that the route calculation failed.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the route calculation failed.

Title: Edge case - Empty route details
Preconditions: User is logged in and tries to start a route with empty details.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a route with empty details.
2. Application sends data for calculation to the server.
3. Server detects empty route details.
4. Server returns an error message to the application.
5. Application informs the user that the route details are invalid.
Expected Result: User receives a message indicating that the route details are invalid.

Title: Edge case - Maximum route length exceeded
Preconditions: User is logged in and tries to start a route exceeding maximum length.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a route with details exceeding maximum length.
2. Application sends data for calculation to the server.
3. Server detects route length exceeds maximum limit.
4. Server returns an error message to the application.
5. Application informs the user that the route length exceeds the maximum allowed.
Expected Result: User receives a message indicating that the route length exceeds the maximum allowed.

Title: Edge case - Minimum route length not met
Preconditions: User is logged in and tries to start a route below minimum length.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a route with details below minimum length.
2. Application sends data for calculation to the server.
3. Server detects route length below minimum limit.
4. Server returns an error message to the application.
5. Application informs the user that the route length is too short.
Expected Result: User receives a message indicating that the route length is too short.

Title: Edge case - Invalid route coordinates
Preconditions: User is logged in and tries to start a route with invalid coordinates.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a route with invalid coordinates.
2. Application sends data for calculation to the server.
3. Server detects invalid route coordinates.
4. Server returns an error message to the application.
5. Application informs the user that the route coordinates are invalid.
Expected Result: User receives a message indicating that the route coordinates are invalid.

Title: Edge case - Duplicate route entry
Preconditions: User is logged in and tries to start a duplicate route.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a route with duplicate details.
2. Application sends data for calculation to the server.
3. Server detects duplicate route entry.
4. Server returns an error message to the application.
5. Application informs the user that the route already exists.
Expected Result: User receives a message indicating that the route already exists.
